---
title: "project01"
name(s):  Jacqueline Caldwell, Micheal Bambha
class: BINF 6250; Fall, 2025
output: 
  github_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    html_preview: false
 
  
The problem:
    Within the program:
      1. Write a function called parse_line that:
        a. Takes a string as an argument
        b. Extract the AF_EXAC data to determine the rarity of the variant
          i. If AF_EXAC is not present, skip the line
          ii.  If the variant is rare (value for the AF_EXAC key < 0.0001)
             return a list of associated diseases in CLNDN
               Do not count the diseases that are:
                    not_specified
                    not_provided
         iii. If the variant is not rare:
             return an empty list
    2.  Write another function called read_file that:
      a. Takes a string as an argument representing the file to be opened
      b. Open the file
      c. Read the file line by line.
         Note: You are expected to do this one line at a time. The reasoning is that 
         if the file is sufficiently large, you may not have the memory available to hold it. 
         So, do not use readlines()!
      d. Passes the line to parse_line
      e. Use a dictionary to count the results given by parse_line to keep a 
      running tally (or count) of the number of times a specific disease is observed
      f. return that dictionary
    4. print the results from read_file when it is complete
    

PSEUDO CODE

CLDN = ""

def parse_line(str1)
# Note string sent will have \n at the end.
  Look for "AF_EXAC" and get value afterwards.
     if value is < 0.0001
      if (ASSOCIATED_DISEASES !=) and (ASSOCIATED_DISEASES != 'not_specified') and (ASSOCIATED_DISEASES != 'not_provided')
        return ASSOCIATED_DISEASES string
      else
        return ""

def read_next_line(fp):
  # fp = file pointer
  # needs to return string with \n at end
     index = seek(fp,1) # read next character in file line
     while ( index != EOF ) and index is not '\n'
        line_string = line_string + single index character
        seek index forward one character
        
    # At this point first character should be the start of     
     If index == EOF 
          return ""   # I may be missing the last line.....
     elif: character is "\n"
          parse_string (line_string)
     else:
          add character to string
          seek to next character.
    return 

def read_file(file_name_str):
  file_pointer = open(file_name_str)
  
  while file_pointer not at EOF:
      next_line = read_next_line(file_pointer) 
      
  close(file_pointer)
  
  
---

```{python}
#!/usr/bin/env python
from pprint import pprint


# Modify this function signature and fill in the details
def parse_line():

def read_file():
  


if __name__ == "__main__":
    pprint(read_file("clinvar_20190923_short.vcf"))

```
